g.data-narration: Welcome to Interthread Communication in Java - a crucial concept for building safe multithreaded applications.
Interthread communication allows threads to cooperate and share data safely using wait, notify, and notifyAll methods.
g.text: A mechanism that allows threads to coordinate and share data safely in
multithreaded programs. Java provides wait(), notify(), notifyAll()
methods from the Object class for this purpose.

g.data-narration: In producer-consumer scenarios, threads must communicate. Without it, the consumer might try to access data before it's produced.
The wait() method makes the current thread release the lock and enter waiting state until another thread notifies it.
The notify() method wakes up exactly one thread that is waiting on the object's monitor. The choice depends on JVM scheduling.
The notifyAll() method wakes up all threads waiting on the object's monitor. They all compete for the lock.
wait, notify, and notifyAll must be called from synchronized context. Otherwise, Java throws IllegalMonitorStateException.
Here's the complete producer-consumer implementation. Notice how wait() and notify() coordinate thread activities.
Spurious wakeups occur without notify being called. Always use wait() inside a while loop to handle this correctly.
g.text: Spurious Wakeups - Why Use While Loop?

g.data-narration: Here's a quick comparison: wait releases lock, notify wakes one thread, notifyAll wakes all threads.
Remember these exam tips: wait() releases lock, use while loop, and thread selection depends on JVM scheduling.
g.text: Thread selection depends on JVM scheduling

g.data-narration: Interthread communication is used in message queues, thread pools, and event systems - essential for modern concurrent applications.
g.text: Master these concepts for building efficient concurrent Java applications!
